reason: Metformin is contraindicated when eGFR is below 30 mL/min/1.73 m2 due to lactic acidosis risk.
SGLT2 inhibitors should not be initiated when eGFR is below 20 mL/min/1.73 m2.
citation: KDIGO 2022 Diabetes in CKD; FDA labeling.

reason: Finerenone is not recommended to be initiated when eGFR is below 25 mL/min/1.73 m2.
Glyburide is renally cleared and carries a high hypoglycemia risk in reduced kidney function; avoid when eGFR is below 60.
Sulfonylureas are sulfonamide derivatives and are contraindicated in patients with a sulfa allergy.
ACE inhibitors can worsen hyperkalemia; avoid initiation when serum potassium exceeds 5.5 mEq/L.
Angiotensin receptor blockers can worsen hyperkalemia; avoid initiation when serum potassium exceeds 5.5 mEq/L.
Finerenone should only be initiated when serum potassium is 5.0 mEq/L or below due to hyperkalemia risk.
Combined ACE inhibitor and ARB therapy (dual RAS blockade) increases the risk of hyperkalemia and acute kidney injury and is not recommended.
Combined ARB and ACE inhibitor therapy (dual RAS blockade) increases the risk of hyperkalemia and acute kidney injury and is not recommended.
DPP-4 inhibitors and GLP-1 receptor agonists act on the same incretin pathway and should not be combined.
Combining a sulfonylurea with insulin substantially increases hypoglycemia risk; use with caution and consider dose reduction.
The patient is already on a medication of this class; recommending another of the same class is duplicate therapy.
